messages.content: Explain why gradient accumulation is useful for fine-tuning on a single GPU.
Gradient accumulation splits a large effective batch into several smaller micro batches. It lowers peak memory while keeping the optimizer update closer to a larger batch run.
What is the practical difference between LoRA and QLoRA?
LoRA trains small adapter weights while the base model stays frozen. QLoRA also freezes the base model, but loads it in 4-bit precision to reduce memory usage further.
